_about: Project-level Claude Code settings — committed to git. Per-user permission overrides live in .claude/settings.local.json (gitignored). Both files are merged at session start; settings.local.json wins on conflict. Filed as #258 Wave 1 (2026-05-29). The first ship used $CLAUDE_FILE_PATH which is the wrong input mechanism (hooks receive JSON on stdin, not env vars); pipe-tested + fixed 2026-05-29.

PostToolUse._about: #258-E (2026-05-29) — auto-fix safe ruff rules on every .py edit. Catches UP041 / I001 / D200 etc. at edit time instead of gate time (~50ms feedback loop vs minutes). Hit this exact friction in the #168 ship: UP041 on `socket.gaierror` required manual fix + retry after gates failed. `--exit-zero` keeps it non-blocking — gate 1 in scripts/run_all_gates.sh stays authoritative for blocking. Restricted to F/E/W/UP/I (safe auto-fixable rules); does NOT touch S security rules (those need human review). The Python one-liner extracts tool_input.file_path from the stdin JSON payload — works on Windows Git Bash without jq.

hooks.command: f=$(python -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null) && case "$f" in *.py) python -m ruff check --fix --select F,E,W,UP,I --exit-zero "$f" 2>&1 | tail -5 ;; esac

PreToolUse._about: #258-F (2026-05-29) — defense against re-introducing the #248 prod-log-leak. `app.py` has a `_RUNNING_UNDER_PYTEST` import-time gate that replaces the module-level `app = create_app()` with a bare Flask() stub when pytest is loaded (preventing DBLogHandler from attaching to prod DB during test runs spawned via the /utilities coverage card — see #229b). Any edit that adds new module-level side effects OUTSIDE that guard re-creates the leak silently. This hook prints a reminder so the operator notices BEFORE the edit lands.

hooks.command: f=$(python -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null) && case "$f" in *app.py) printf '\033[33m\xe2\x9a\xa0  Editing app.py — module-level side effects are gated by _RUNNING_UNDER_PYTEST (see line ~880). Adding side effects outside that guard re-introduces the #248 prod-log-leak.\033[0m\n' >&2 ;; esac
